behavior_summary: Provides item-specific behavior for a magic scroll item. When examined (U8P_MAGSCROL::look), the script reads the scroll's quality/type byte and presents a short human-readable name to the player via the item's bark.
When used (use helper), the script reads the same quality/type byte and spawns a corresponding SCROLL1/SCROLL2 effect script entry point to perform the scroll's in-game effect.

Known quality values are mapped to specific descriptive strings (for U8P_MAGSCROL::look) and to specific SCROLL1/SCROLL2 entry addresses (for use). If the quality byte is not matched, the script falls back to generic defaults (generic 'magic scroll' text or a default spawn target).
Item::getQuality is the single source of truth for scroll identity; Item::bark reports names to the player and spawn starts the effect implementation.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MAGSCROL::look: fetch quality via Item::getQuality -> switch/compare on quality byte -> call Item::bark(pid, <string>, item) with a hard-coded string for matched values -> fall back to generic strings when no match.

U8P_MAGSCROL::use: fetch quality via Item::getQuality -> switch/compare on quality byte -> select and pass a SCROLL1 or SCROLL2 entry address to spawn(pid, address) to create the effect process -> default spawn target used if no mapping matches.
Dispatch is quality-driven: the single byte returned by Item::getQuality determines both the player-visible name and which effect implementation is executed.
open_questions: Complete mapping of all possible quality byte values to descriptive strings and spawn targets — only several concrete mappings were observed (examples include 0x00, 0x01, 0x32..0x37 and others).
Exact semantics and required parameters of the spawned SCROLL1/SCROLL2 entry points (their internal effects are external to this script and only inferred).
Whether the pid passed into Item::bark is always the viewing player's PID or sometimes a different process — inferred as the viewer's PID but not explicitly documented here.
Whether any global flags or item-info fields are modified by these handlers beyond the spawn/bark operations (none observed in disassembly excerpt).

main_function.name: U8P_MAGSCROL::look
main_function.purpose: When the scroll is examined by a player/actor, read the scroll's quality/type byte and present a short human-readable scroll name via Item::bark. Use a lookup of known quality codes and fall back to generic labels when unknown.
entry_conditions: Event 0: the 'U8P_MAGSCROL::look' (examine) event, triggered when an actor inspects the scroll item.

function_map.name: U8P_MAGSCROL::look
function_map.role: Handle the item's 'U8P_MAGSCROL::look' event: read quality and bark an appropriate descriptive string to the viewer.

function_map.name: U8P_MAGSCROL::use
function_map.role: Handle the item's 'use' event: read quality and spawn the corresponding scroll-effect script (SCROLL1/SCROLL2 entry points).

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: Engine/Item API call used to retrieve the scroll's quality/type byte (dispatch key).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/Item API call used to display/speak a short text string from the item to the player/process.

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: Runtime/engine call used to start another usecode script/process (used by U8P_MAGSCROL::use to invoke SCROLL1/SCROLL2 entry points).

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the scroll's quality/type byte to decide which descriptive string to show.
Present the chosen human-readable scroll name to the player/process that triggered the U8P_MAGSCROL::look event (passed pid and the item instance).
Obtain the scroll's quality/type byte to determine which effect script to spawn.
Start the appropriate SCROLL1 or SCROLL2 script entry point that implements the scroll's effect, passing the initiating pid and the target entry address.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides item-specific behavior for a magic scroll item. When examined (U8P_MAGSCROL::look), the script reads the scroll's quality/type byte and presents a short human-readable name to the player via the item's bark.
